LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS MAXXAM INC. AND CHARLES E. HURWITZ'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - OVERRULED; TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND THAT THE CLAIMS AT ISSUE ARE SUBJECT TO THE PUBLIC DISCLOSURE LIMITATION. THE ISSUE OF EXHAUSTION OF INTERNAL REMEDIES IS A QUESTION OF FACT, NOT FOR DEMURRER. ON THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT, IT IS NOT APPARENT THAT THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS BARS THE CLAIMS. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND PERSUASIVE DEFENDANTS' ARGUMENTS THAT THERE WAS NO "CLAIM" AND THAT SYP WAS NOT ESSENTIAL TO THE PURCHASE. FINALLY, FRAUD AND ALTER EGO ARE SUFFICIENTLY PLED. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CHARLES HURWITZ AND MAXXAM INC.'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE AND REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THIS MOTION IS BROUGHT BY AN INDIVIDUAL AND CORPORATION. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL RESIDES AND HAS HIS PRINCIPLE PLACE OF BUSINESS IN HOUSTON, TEXAS. THE CORPORATION IS ALLEGED TO BE A DELAWARE CORPORATION WITH ITS PRINCIPLE PLACE OF BUSINESS IN HOUSTON, TEXAS. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT THESE DEFENDANTS HAVE SUBMITTED A STATEMENT DESIGNATING THE COUNTY IN WHICH EITHER MAINTAINS ITS PRINCIPLE LOCAL OFFICE. AS A CONSEQUENCE, THE CORPORATION MAY BE SUED IN ANY COUNTY IN CALIFORNIA. EASTON V. SUPERIOR COURT (1970) 12 CAL.APP.3D 243, 246-247. A CORPORATION'S MOTION TO TRANSFER CAN ONLY BE BASED ON ITS PRINCIPLE PLACE. KARSON INDUSTRIES, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1969) 273 CAL.APP.2D 7, 8-9. IT, THEREFORE FOLLOWS, THAT THE MOTION CANNOT BE BASED ON THE BUSINESS OF THE OTHER DEFENDANTS, PARTICULARLY IN THIS CASE WHERE SAID DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT APPEARED, HAVE FILED FOR BANKRUPTCY AND HAVE OBTAINED A STAY. CC 395 IS THE APPLICABLE VENUE PROVISION BASED ON THE COMPLAINT'S ALLEGATIONS. AS TO THE MOVING DEFENDANTS, THE FOLLOWING LANGUAGE IS APPLICABLE: "IF NONE OF THE DEFENDANTS RESIDE IN THE STATE, 'THE ACTION MAY BE TRIED IN THE SUPERIOR COURT IN ANY COUNTY THAT THE PLAINTIFF MAY DESIGNATE IN HIS OR HER COMPLAINT." FOR THE REASONS STATED, THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT DECLINES TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE DOCUMENTS REQUESTED BY DEFENDANTS. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANTS INSIST THAT THE FACTS SUPPORTING THE ALLEGED FRAUD ARE DISCLOSED IN EXHIBITS A AND B, DEFENDANTS DO NOT POINT TO SPECIFIC PAGES IN THE LENGTHY EXHIBIT THAT SETS FORTH THE FACTS UPON WHICH DEFENDANTS RELY. THE COURT GRANTS JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS BASED ON THE DECISION IN PEOPLE EX REL. GALLEGOS V. PACIFIC LUMBER CO. (2008) 158 CAL.AP.4TH 950. THAT DECISION HOLDS THAT THE NOERR-PENNINGTON DOCTRINE IS APPLICABLE TO DEFENDANTS' SUBMISSION OF THE SUSTAINED YIELD PLAN ("SPY") TO THE STATE. GALLEGOS ADDRESSED ANOTHER PART OF THE PLAN, BUT IN GALLEGOS, DEFENDANTS ALSO WERE CHARGED WITH USING IMPROPER TACTICS TO OBTAIN PERMISSION TO HARVEST CERTAIN LEVELS OF TIMBER. PLAINTIFFS CONTEND THAT A FALSE CLAIMS ACT CAUSE OF ACTION IS FUNDAMENTALLY DIFFERENT FROM AN UNFAIR COMPETITION CLAIM BECAUSE THE FALSE CLAIMS ACT INVOLVES THE PUBLIC FISC. THE ARGUMENT IS NOT SUPPORTED BY CASE LAW. MOREOVER, THE NOERR-PENNINGTON DOCTRINE PROMOTES VIGOROUS ADVOCACY TO GOVERNMENTAL BODIES; IT IS BASED ON PRINCIPLES SIMILAR TO CIVIL CODE SECTION 47 THAT PROTECTS VIRTUALLY ALL FORMS OF CONDUCT IN THE CONTEXT OF LITIGATION. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND PLAINTIFFS' DISTINCTION TO BE PERSUASIVE. NOR IS THE COURT CONCERNED THAT SUCH A RULING WOULD UNDERMINE THE FALSE CLAIMS ACT. ALTHOUGH THE COURT REJECTED THE CONTENTION THAT PLAINTIFFS HAD NOT PLEAD A FALSE CLAIM, THE RULING WAS BASED ON A DEMURRER AND THE CLAIM ALLEGED IS FAR FROM A GARDEN VARIETY FALSE CLAIM MADE TO A GOVERNMENTAL ENTITY. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO AMEND WAS DENIED. THE COURT WILL ISSUE A MINUTE ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-14-2007 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MAXXAM INC. and CHARLES E. HURWITZ'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: Barbara L. Lyons, Esq. of Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy 650/697-6000 for pltf & opposing party; Edgar B. Washburn, Esq. of Morrison Foerster LLP 268-7860 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Overruled; ten days to answer. The Court does not find that the claims at issue are subject to the public disclosure limitation. The issue of exhaustion of internal remedies is a question of fact, not for demurrer. On the face of the complaint, it is not apparent that the statute of limitations bars the claims. The Court does not find persuasive defendants' arguments that there was no "claim" and that SYP was not essential to the purchase. Finally, fraud and alter ego are sufficiently pled. Plaintiff to submit proposed form of order. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Aug-01-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CHARLES HURWITZ AND MAXXAM INC.'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE AND REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE.
APPEARANCES: JOSEPH COTCHETT, ESQUIRE AND BARBARA LYONS, ESQUIRE (COTCHETT PITRE SIMON AND McCARTHY, 650.697.6000) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFFS. THERE ARE NO OTHER APPEARANCES.
RULING: COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: This motion is brought by an individual and corporation. The complaint alleges that the individual resides and has his principle place of business in Houston, Texas. The corporation is alleged to be a Delaware corporation with its principle place of business in Houston, Texas. There is no evidence that these defendants have submitted a statement designating the county in which either maintains its principle local office. As a consequence, the corporation may be sued in any county in California. Easton v. Superior Court (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 246-247.
A corporation's motion to transfer can only be based on its principle place. Karson Industries, Inc. v. Superior Court (1969) 273 Cal.App.2d 7, 8-9. It, therefore follows, that the motion cannot be based on the business of the other defendants, particularly in this case where said defendants have not appeared, have filed for bankruptcy and have obtained a stay.
CC 395 is the applicable venue provision based on the complaint's allegations. As to the moving defendants, the following language is applicable: "If none of the defendants reside in the state, 'the action may be tried in the superior court in any county that the plaintiff may designate in his or her complaint."
For the reasons stated, the motion is denied. The request for attorneys' fees is denied. Plaintiff's counsel to submit a form of order. (302/PJM/CB-X) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
